{"query":"Wimbledon 2026 protest Save Wimbledon Park June 2026 residents open space demonstration tournament start coverage","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1882,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c70y1re4ppwo","title":"Wimbledon expansion plans draw protests at start of tournament","content":"A group opposing plans to expand the Wimbledon tennis complex held a protest outside the ground as the tournament got under way on Monday."},{"url":"https://www.cjag.org/2026/07/06/wimbledon-expansion-protesters-claim-that-the-tennis-club-is-not-listening/","title":"Wimbledon expansion: Protesters claim that the Tennis Club is not listening – Clapham Junction Insider – Local Democracy Reporting","content":"Others would think this is evidence of poor organisation, unseen at other Grand Slam tournaments (the idea of hundreds of people camping on the Boulevard d’Auteuil in Paris is indeed laughable).
But taking over most of the park for their “organised queue” each summer is not enough for the All England Lawn Tennis Club (AELTC).
“This is the public park for the people of Merton.
This is Wimbledon public park, and our local space is being taken over.
People waiting for tickets at 6:30am on Monday – Credit: CJI
“This would still be here, even if they’ve got all of their plans, and they built everything they want.
At the end of April, SWP organised a presentation of an alternative plan.
The AELTC says it needs more capacity to secure the Championships’ long-term future, which is why it wants the larger new show-court complex and more practice/competition courts.
The pressure Rees is trying to address is the need for more capacity and smoother operations during the Championships.
In a previous message sent to CJI last year, the AELTC claimed to have spoken to more than 10,000 people as part of its consultation and information events, including more than 100 guided tours of the proposed southern parkland, various open days, “meet the expert” events, and residents’ groups.
This past April, Mr Justice Thompsell ruled comprehensively against SWP, finding on three separate grounds that no such trust had ever existed — essentially because the golf course had always been run as a private club and never opened to the public, so nothing was ever “dedicated” to public use in the first place."},{"url":"https://www.lta.org.uk/play-your-way-to-wimbledon/","title":"Play Your Way to Wimbledon | Entry & format guide | LTA","content":"Events will run from 2 March – 24 May 2026.
Players can only enter a competition in their play county, for example, if they play in Yorkshire, they can only enter a Play Your Way to Wimbledon event in Yorkshire.
Competitions are hosted at any LTA registered venues, as well as Schools (juniors only) in England, Scotland, Wales, Channel Islands and Isle of Man.
Area Final winners are then invited to compete in the National Finals at Wimbledon from 2 - 8 August 2026.
For 2026, this tournament is only open to ii1 classified players.
Please note, County Finals in Scotland will be played over the weekend of 13 and 14 June.
Area Finals will be taking place over the weekend of 20 and 21 June.
14U Singles – 2 – 6 August
18U Doubles - 4 – 7 August"}]}
